title: HTML Tokenization, Tree Construction, goquery, and Parser Differentials · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds budgeted Go HTML tree adapter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of budgeted Go HTML tree adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.
Construct goquery documents from admitted readers and preserve base, source, parser, and response identity outside selections.
Test implied elements, misnesting, tables, templates, foreign content, comments, duplicate attributes, and deep input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Token Vs Tree
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 1179 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove tokenizer versus tree choice, goquery documents, malformed repair, browser differentials, and budgets; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Token Vs Tree.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Token Vs Tree, keep Token Vs Tree connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Node Work Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-node-work-budget-1fo3qn" that uses case 6367 to prove tokenizer versus tree choice, goquery documents, malformed repair, browser differentials, and budgets. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
